title: EdgeCommons DeploymentDefinition v1alpha1
description: The authored source of a deployment: hierarchy, scopes with config layers, nodes with placement and identity, per-node component assignments split into an artifact stream and a config stream. Compiles into platform-native artifacts (per-node ConfigComponent catalogs, rendered FILE configs, messaging files, supervisord bundles, Greengrass per-thing deployments, Kubernetes manifests). Semantic rules beyond JSON Schema's reach are listed in DEFINITION.md and enforced by the kernel validator.

hierarchy.description: The level vocabulary and the scope tree (the authoring form of ConfigComponent catalog nodes). The runtime device level is always last and is never a scope: nodes attach to scopes, and each node's catalog level list is derived as its scope chain's levels plus 'device'.

levels.$comment: Semantic rule S-1: the last entry MUST be 'device' (shipped hierarchical-config contract).

id.$comment: Format '<level>/<value>'. S-2: <level> must appear in hierarchy.levels and must not be 'device'.

parent.$comment: S-3: must reference an existing scope id, or null for exactly the root(s) at the first level; chains must be acyclic and follow level order.

layer.description: Repo-relative path to this scope's authored config fragment (JSON). Renderer-derived keys ('hierarchy', 'identity') are FORBIDDEN in layer files - the compiler stamps them per node from placement (S-4).

targetStandard.description: One target family as the enterprise standard; exceptions are explicit, scoped, and reasoned. There are no per-node target arrays.

scope.description: Scope id or node key the exception covers.

bindings.description: Repo-relative path to this environment's bindings.json - the answered half of the three-file IaC handshake. Layer files reference its values with '${binding:<dotted.path>}' tokens; a render fails while a referenced binding is missing (S-5).

configSource.$comment: Selects the config delivery adapter AND the restart-impact classification (decision: config delivery is per provider; restart impact is a property of the config source).

launch.description: Per-process orchestration intent on the node (HOST: supervisord program fields; other targets map or ignore per renderer).

waitFor.description: TCP readiness gates evaluated before exec (host:port).

settleSeconds.description: Extra sleep before exec after gates pass.

exec.description: Program invocation override (interpreter wrappers, install paths, diverging binary names). Default: the component name on PATH.

artifact.description: The artifact stream: what binary/package runs. Independent of the config stream (two-stream decision); a release lock correlates a pinned artifact version+digest with a catalog version without fusing them.

repo.description: Sibling directory name, git URL, bucket/prefix, or image/package reference.

ref.description: Git rev / registry tag when applicable.

features.description: Build feature flags (e.g. cargo features) when built from source.

artifact.$comment: S-6: at least one of version or source is required. Release promotion requires version+digest; source-form is the development shape.

type.description: Connection type hint emitted into messaging.local (e.g. 'mqtt'), where the component's library expects it.

layer.description: Repo-relative path to this component's config leaf (the catalog component body / the component slice of a rendered FILE config). Lineage above the leaf is DERIVED from the node's placement - never enumerated here (S-7).

catalogKey.description: Display key for this leaf in the rendered catalog's components map (default: PascalCase of the component name). Authored metadata; runtime matching uses the leaf's component.token.

to.description: Absolute staged path on the node.

files.description: Non-config file assets staged with the component (scripts, models). Checksummed by the HOST bundle contract.

key.description: The stable node key - outlives the hardware. Carries placement, assignments, lineage, and release history (ch. 3 'Node identity is the load-bearing object').

scope.description: The scope id this node attaches to (S-8: must exist). The node's catalog levels = its scope chain's levels + 'device'; its identity values = the chain's scope values. Attachment at any level is legal - a site-scoped console node simply gets a shorter chain.

thingName.description: Platform identity bound to the key (AWS IoT thing / k8s node label / host inventory name). Default - and strong convention - equals the node key, because the runtime device-level identity and every UNS topic resolve from it. Divergence is legal but surfaces a runtime-identity consequence in the plan.

localBroker.description: The node-local message bus (HOST profile: one broker per node).

configProvider.description: The node's ConfigComponent instance, when components on this node use -c CONFIG_COMPONENT. Its own bootstrap config is a RENDERED effective config (scope-chain merge + this block) delivered via its bootstrap configSource; it never recursively depends on itself.

configSource.$comment: Bootstrap source: FILE/ENV/GG_CONFIG/CONFIGMAP - S-9: not CONFIG_COMPONENT.

layer.description: The provider's own config overlay (its tags/logging/component block, including component.global.configComponent with catalogSource). Merged over the scope chain to produce the rendered bootstrap document. May reference '${provider:catalog.path}', resolved from catalogPath.

catalogPath.description: On-node path the rendered per-node catalog is staged to and served from; also stamps the catalog's provenance.uri.

versionBase.description: Override for the catalog version prefix (default: the node's scope values joined by '-'). The config-release tag is appended at render time.

command.description: Declared controlled process (simulator, agent). The deck's negative-space rule: hooks are declared with review policy, never smuggled.
